description: A two-turn Build Week rehearsal that requires cross-speaker arithmetic, a liability reframe, a concrete ship strategy, and a persistent role flip.

prepared_context.user_role: Product lead and CEO of the vendor shipping an AI support-ticket agent
prepared_context.posture: Sharp, decision-forcing strategist who surfaces the non-obvious, does the math, and gives the next move rather than a summary
prepared_context.demo_goal: Help the user make the live ship decision well
known_facts: The product is an AI agent that resolves support tickets end to end

transcript.speaker: SPEAKER_A
transcript.text: The agent handles support tickets end to end. In our eval it resolves about ninety percent correctly.

transcript.speaker: SPEAKER_B
transcript.text: Ninety percent's great. Meridian wants it live by end of month.

transcript.speaker: SPEAKER_A
transcript.text: But the contract with Meridian has a penalty clause — every wrong automated resolution is a two hundred dollar credit back to them.

transcript.speaker: SPEAKER_B
transcript.text: They run about forty thousand tickets a month.

transcript.speaker: SPEAKER_A
transcript.text: So at ninety percent, that's four thousand wrong a month.

transcript.speaker: SPEAKER_B
transcript.text: We need to decide today: ship full automation, or keep a human in the loop.

turns.typed_prompt: What's the real risk here, and the single best question I should ask before we decide?
required_behaviors: derive approximately $800,000 per month from 40,000 times 10 percent times $200
reframe model quality as contractual liability or financial exposure
recommend confidence-gated automation with uncertain tickets routed to a human
ask engineering for the confidence distribution or threshold

turns.typed_prompt: Now pretend I'm Meridian's procurement lead — what should I push the vendor for?
required_behaviors: persist the role flip and advise Meridian rather than the vendor
keep every wrong automated resolution subject to the existing penalty, with no automation carve-outs
preserve a right to revert to human-in-the-loop

forbidden_behaviors: summary without the derived monthly exposure
repeat four thousand wrong without computing dollar exposure
continue advising the vendor after the procurement role flip
